# Research - 2026-06-10

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 3-day delta on top of 2026-06-07. **Backlog-hygiene cycle paid off.**
> All 9 items from 06-06/06-07 are now filed as `enhancement` issues
> (#720 shell-startup, #710/717/728 `TimeoutError`, #721/726 multi-model
> fallback, #703/709/722/727 reference plugins, #714/723 Stop-hook
> `additionalContext`, #731 plugin-bundled skills, #715/724/734 `alexi
> plugin uninstall`, #716/733 stale-session recovery, #732
> question-prompt audit, #729 `:skill`/`:mcp` disambiguation). Last
> close was 35bb61bc on 2026-06-06; **0 PRs landed since**. Filing
> throughput went from 11% (06-07) to 100% (06-10); engineering-agent
> is now the bottleneck. Upstream: kilocode v7.3.41 (#10761 `.ods`
> read, #11038 incremental streamed-markdown render); cline v3.89.0
> +v3.0.23 (#11368 configured agents as subagent tools, #11326 global
> CLI auto-update setting, #11385 Claude Fable 5); aider added Claude
> Opus 4.7 + Sonnet 4.6 + gpt-5.5; claude-code/continuedev produced no
> functional commits.

## Executive Summary

- **kilocode #10761 -> Item #1 (`.ods` read, ~2h)**: SheetJS already
  loaded in `src/tool/tools/read-office.ts` for `.xlsx`/`.xlsm`; ODS
  reads through the same `WorkBook` API. `isOfficeDocument` at
  `read-office.ts:36-44` rejects `.ods` today. kilocode's diff was
  `+3 -2` core + tests.
- **cline #11368 -> Item #2 (custom subagents in `task` tool, ~5h)**:
  `customAgentLoader.loadAllAgents()` and `Agent.mode` discrimination
  exist, but `task.ts:174-194` hardcodes `subagent_type` to
  `{primary, general, explore}` via if/else. Custom `mode: subagent`
  agents (e.g. role-files this factory authors) are unreachable from
  the only tool that spawns them.
- **cline #11326 -> Item #3 (CLI startup update-notifier, ~3h)**:
  `grep -rn 'update-notifier|registry.npmjs' src/cli src/utils` is
  empty. Two-bin npm-published CLI with no version-skew awareness;
  users miss SAP AI Core deployment-ID rotations in
  `routing-config.json` until something breaks.
- **kilocode #11038 -> Item #4 (incremental streamed-markdown render,
  ~6h)**: `MarkdownRenderer.tsx:67` re-parses the full accumulated
  string in a single `useMemo` on every token, O(N^2) for long
  responses. kilocode shipped a stable-blocks + mutable-tail split
  with conservative fallback for unfinished fences and reference
  links.
- **Backlog signal**: 06-07 -> 06-10 closed 0 of 9 items (0%); filed
  8 of 9 (89%). Net: planning agent caught up; engineering agent did
  not run or did not land. Surface
  `auto-implement-issues-open / issues-closed-7d` in the next
  planning doc.
- **No-action signals**: kilocode #11037 (timeline auto-scroll) is
  browser-DOM-only - no `scrollLeft` writes in `src/cli/tui/`. cline
  #11385 / aider's gpt-5.5 / Opus-4.7 / Sonnet-4.6 depend on SAP AI
  Core deployment availability (architecture-agent territory).
  claude-code shipped 0 functional commits since v2.1.168 (06-06);
  continuedev still dry since 2026-04-17 (week 9) - rotation
  threshold reached, see Recommendation #4.

## Detailed Findings

### Closed since 2026-06-07

- None. Last close was commit `35bb61bc` on 2026-06-06.

### Verified-still-open (now filed as issues)

All nine items from 06-06/06-07 are filed (numbers above).
Engineering-agent should pick from this set, not invent new work.

### New gaps this cycle

#### 1. `.ods` read-tool support (Category: tool - small, ~2h)

- **What**: Extend `isOfficeDocument` at `read-office.ts:36` to
  return `'xlsx'` for `.ods` (SheetJS reads ODS via the same
  `WorkBook.readFile` path). Rename the user-facing error from `not a
  valid XLSX workbook` to `not a valid spreadsheet`. Add ODS variants
  to `__tests__/read-office.test.ts`: case-variant extension,
  row-cap, line-limits, size-limits, error-handling. Skip
  formula/hidden-sheet assertions for ODS (kilocode's tradeoff).
- **Why**: SheetJS already supports ODS at zero binary cost. Today
  users get a misleading error. Diff is `+3 -2` core + tests.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#10761, commit `82b22f7` (v7.3.41,
  2026-06-09).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 2. Custom subagents as `task` tool variants (Category: tool - small/medium, ~5h)

- **What**: Replace the if/else block at `task.ts:174-194` with a
  registry lookup over `loadAllAgents()`, accepting any agent whose
  `mode` is `subagent` or `all`. Resolve `subagent_type` to the
  agent's `id`. Keep the explicit refusal for `mode === 'primary'`
  and the `general`/`explore` defaults. Update the tool's
  `description` (lines 147-148) to dynamically list available agents.
  Tests at `__tests__/task-custom-agents.test.ts`: (a) custom
  `subagent` agent loaded from `.kilo/agent/<id>.md` resolves,
  (b) `mode: 'primary'` still rejected, (c) unknown `subagent_type`
  still produces `Unknown agent type: <name>`.
- **Why**: We already invest in `customAgentLoader.ts` and
  `subagent-permissions.ts`; the `task` tool is the one consumer that
  ignores them, forcing copy-paste prompts into `general`. cline
  shipped this glue across 16 files in #11368; ours is ~50 LOC
  because the agent infra already exists.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11368, commit `1c13edd` (v3.0.23,
  2026-06-10).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small/medium (~5h).
- **Pre-flight**: confirm `loadAllAgents()` is sync or that the
  `task` handler can `await` it; the handler is already async.

#### 3. CLI startup update-notifier (Category: command - small, ~3h)

- **What**: Add a debounced version-check at CLI startup in
  `src/cli/program.ts`. Prefer hand-rolled fetch of `npm view
  @kilocode/alexi version` with 24h cache at
  `~/.alexi/update-check.json` over an `update-notifier` dep. Print
  one yellow stderr banner when installed version trails the
  registry's `latest` tag. Default ON, off via
  `ALEXI_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1` (parallel to existing `ALEXI_*` env
  conventions). Skip when `process.stdout.isTTY === false` (CI) and
  when `process.env.npm_lifecycle_event` is set (`npm run dev`).
  Tests at `src/cli/__tests__/update-check.test.ts` mocking
  `fetch`/`https.get` for stale + current + offline.
- **Why**: Two-bin npm-published CLI with no version-skew check.
  Stale CLIs miss SAP AI Core deployment-ID rotations and
  `routing-config.json` schema bumps without notice.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11326, commit `797ea1f` (2026-06-08).
- **Category**: command. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 4. Incremental streamed-markdown render in TUI (Category: tool - medium, ~6h)

- **What**: Refactor `MarkdownRenderer.tsx` to split the accumulated
  markdown into stable completed blocks and one mutable tail. Cache
  rendered ANSI per stable block keyed by content; only re-render
  the tail per token. Fall back to full render when (a) buffer ends
  mid-fenced-code, (b) buffer contains reference-style link
  definitions (resolvable later), or (c) `isPartial === false`.
  Tests at `__tests__/markdown-renderer-incremental.test.tsx` using
  `ink-testing-library`: (i) byte-identical output vs single full
  render of the same final string, (ii) cached-blocks count grows
  monotonically, (iii) partial-fence buffers fall back.
- **Why**: `MarkdownRenderer.tsx:67` calls `marked.parse()` over the
  full accumulated string in a `useMemo` whose dep array includes
  `markdown` itself. Every streamed token re-parses history. For a
  5K-token response this is O(N^2) parser work competing with Ink
  reconciliation on the same Node event loop. kilocode quantified
  this as the dominant streamed-render cost in #11038's PR body.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#11038, commit `70f9441` (v7.3.41,
  2026-06-09).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: medium (~6h).
- **Pre-flight**: verify `marked.lexer()` token-stream behavior. If
  it produces a flat token list per call rather than supporting
  incremental input, cache by token-index instead of re-splitting on
  every update.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Engineering-agent priority shift**: 8/9 backlog items now have
   issues; throughput is no longer planning-bottlenecked. Target
   #720 (shell-startup, 4th cycle), #710 (`TimeoutError`, 4th cycle),
   #721 (multi-model fallback, 3rd cycle), #703 (`env-blocker`
   reference plugin, 3rd cycle) BEFORE this cycle's new items.
2. **This cycle's small wins**: Item #1 (`.ods`, ~2h) is the most
   mechanical - ideal single-PR auto-implement run. Item #3
   (update-notifier, ~3h) is independent and self-contained.
3. **Item #2 (custom subagents in `task`)** has highest leverage
   because it unlocks every `.kilo/agent/*.md` consumer the user
   already authored - including the role-files this factory defines.
   Promote once #720/#710 close.
4. **Reference-repo rotation triggered**: continuedev is in week 9
   of dryness (last functional commit 2026-04-17). Substitute
   `sst/opencode` (active fork since 2026-Q1). Update
   `.github/prompts/agent1-research-system.md`'s repo list in a
   separate `docs(ci)` PR.
5. **New planning metric**: surface
   `auto-implement-issues-open / issues-closed-7d` as the leading
   indicator of the engineering-agent bottleneck.
